source: Golf Channel / Golf Monthly / National Club Golfer

data.value: World No. 1 is the heavy favorite across all four 2026 majors, having won two majors in 2025 (PGA Championship, The Open). Has never finished outside the top 20 in six Augusta appearances.
McIlroy defending the Masters seeks a rare back-to-back victory — something no player has accomplished since Tiger Woods in 2002. Faces enormous expectations and a historically stacked field.
Won the Genesis Invitational in February 2026 and analysts believe his first major title is a matter of when, not if. Most likely new major champion in 2026 per Golf Monthly analysts.
Under immense pressure heading into 2026 after a disappointing 2025 major season. Analysts see redemption as his storyline but temper expectations given LIV transition challenges.
English crowd favorite at The Open (Royal Birkdale, near his hometown Southport) at +1400. Links style suits his game and local support could be a factor.
